method_atom: an exact one-to-one correspondence (bijection) between the discrete set of stationary/ground configurations of one class of systems — characterized as the critical points of an effective potential functional — and the complete set of eigenstates of an exactly-solvable system whose spectrum is fixed by a finite set of coupled algebraic quantization conditions of Bethe type. Deforming the underlying geometry/parameters (adding a periodic direction, a doubly-periodic direction, or an extra dimension) systematically deforms these quantization conditions into rational, trigonometric, and elliptic variants. A hidden infinite-dimensional (quantum-group / Yangian-type) algebra acts as a symmetry unifying an entire family of such models.
object_atom: the supersymmetric vacua of two- (and, upon compactification, three- and four-) dimensional N=2 supersymmetric gauge theories with matter hypermultiplets, placed in correspondence with quantum integrable spin chains and lattice models (Heisenberg XXX, XXZ, XYZ / eight-vertex, the Hubbard model, and the Sinh-Gordon and nonlinear Schroedinger models) of theoretical high-energy and statistical physics.
brief_reason: The transferable content is the abstract bijection-between-critical-points-and-integrable-spectra-via-Bethe-equations plus its quantum-group symmetry; what is domain-specific is the concrete pairing of N=2 gauge-theory vacua with particular physics spin chains, which are somewhat fused since the correspondence is stated as a bridge between these two specific object classes.
